raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "asserting a universal causal law linking all suffering to mental error, functioning as moral/philosophical instruction",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "all suffering has an identifiable mental cause",
    "thoughts are the sole or primary origin of suffering",
    "a clear distinction exists between 'right' and 'wrong' thought",
    "eliminating wrong thought would eliminate suffering"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "logically consistent within a closed mental-causation ontology but tautological once 'wrong thought' is defined post hoc by the presence of suffering",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "the audience accepts radical mental causation, 'wrong thought' can be reliably identified and corrected, and focusing on cognition produces measurable relief without ignoring external constraints",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "suffering arises from factors independent of individual cognition (injury, disease, structural violence, random events) or when 'wrong thought' remains undefined and unfalsifiable"
}
